briefdescription.para: Allocates memory for co_tpdo_t object using allocator from can_net_t.
Frees memory allocated for co_tpdo_t object.
Initializes co_tpdo_t object.
Finalizes co_tpdo_t object.
Initializes the CAN frame receiver of a Transmit-PDO service.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked when one of the TPDO communication parameters (objects 1800..19FF) is updated.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the CAN timer for events of a Transmit-PDO service.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked when one of the TPDO communication parameters (objects 1800..19FF) is updated.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the CAN timer for the synchronous time window of a Transmit-PDO service.
The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen objects 1800..19FF (TPDO communication parameter).
The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen objects 1A00..1BFF (TPDO mapping parameter).
The CAN receive callback function for a Transmit-PDO service.
The CAN timer callback function for events of a Transmit-PDO service.
The CAN timer callback function for the synchronous time window of a Transmit-PDO service.
The default sampling indication function.
Initializes a CAN frame to be sent by a Transmit-PDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
a pointer to the CAN frame to be initialized.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the indication function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: Sends a CAN frame from a Transmit-PDO service and invokes the indication function.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
a pointer to the CAN frame to be sent.

briefdescription.para: Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the co_tpdo_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the co_tpdo_t structure.
Creates a new CANopen Transmit-PDO service.

detaileddescription.para: The service is stopped after creation and SHOULD be started with co_tpdo_start() before use.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network.
a pointer to a CANopen device describing the server.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]). The PDO communication and mapping parameter records MUST exist in the object dictionary of dev.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new Transmit-PDO service, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CANopen Transmit-PDO service.
Starts a Transmit-PDO service.
Stops a Transmit-PDO service.
Returns true if the specified Transmit-PDO service is stopped, and false if not.
Returns a pointer to the CAN network of a Transmit-PDO.
Returns a pointer to the CANopen device of a Transmit-PDO.
Returns the PDO number of a Transmit-PDO.
Returns a pointer to the PDO communication parameter record of a Transmit-PDO.
Returns a pointer to the PDO mapping parameter record of a Transmit-PDO.
Retrieves the indication function invoked when a Transmit-PDO is sent or an error occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a Transmit-PDO is sent or an error occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a Transmit-PDO starts sampling after the reception of a SYNC event.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a Transmit-PDO starts sampling after the reception of a SYNC event.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Triggers the transmission of an acyclic or event-driven PDO.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns an error if the inhibit time has not yet elapsed.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Triggers the transmission of a synchronous PDO.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
the counter value (in the range [0..240]).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Indicates the result of the sampling step after the reception of a SYNC event.

detaileddescription.para: This function MUST be called upon completion.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.
the SDO abort code (0 on success). The PDO is not sent if ac != 0.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the time at which the next event-driven TPDO may be sent.
This file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the implementation of the Transmit-PDO functions.

detaileddescription.para:
The implementation follows CiA 301 version 4.2.0. See section 7.2.2 for the definition of the PDO services and protocols. The following objects determine the behavior of TPDOs:
1007: Synchronous window length
1800..19FF: TPDO communication parameter
1A00..1BFF: TPDO mapping parameter See table 72 for a description of the transmission type in the communication parameters and table 73 for a description of the mapping parameters.
